name: Joseph Glaser
bio: Joseph Glaser is a composer and sound artist whose music creates space for performers and audiences alike to have out-of-the-ordinary experiences. Joseph likes to interrogate the form and function of a concert setting by playing with chance/indeterminacy, injecting elements of theatre and play, and ultimately allowing a piece of music to live beyond a concept or strict set of instructions. In Joseph’s work, the fundamental blocks of a composition is not sound but the performers and their bodies emphasizing the physical nature of performing live music.

name: Sook-Yin Lee
bio:
Sook-Yin Lee, multimedia artist, engineers and produces music. Her album "jooj two," reached #1 on the Earshot national campus music chart. Her songs have been covered by Neko Case, and featured in horror movies INFINITY POOL and ANTIVIRAL. Lee played with experimental legends CCMC, John Oswald, Fred Frith, African-American scholar and poet Fred Moten.

An iconic MuchMusic VJ, and CBC broadcaster, Lee starred in SHORTBUS. Her award-winning feature films include, YEAR OF THE CARNIVORE and OCTAVIO IS DEAD!  She created UNSAFE, on censorship and artistic freedom for Canadian Stage. Her latest movie, the Canadian Screen Award-winning PAYING FOR IT was selected as one of Canada's Top Ten Films.

name: Connor Bennett
bio: Connor Bennett is a musician, craftsperson, and arts organizer in Hamilton. They play saxophone, flute, and whistle in a variety of musical contexts, mostly focused on improvisation (Current projects include: Tidal Pool; Blue Moss; Madam Sad; Horseman, Pass By; Ylang Ylang; Ula Lume; Ghost Variables). Their craft is in custom mouthpiece making for woodwinds. Connor has been involved with Zula Music and Arts Collective for many years and is also a founding member of the Hamilton Audio Visual Node (HAVN) and has worked there in collaboration with many other artists in the curation and organization of concerts, art shows, events, and series.

name: Naomi Sky
bio: Ashley Naomi (she/her), is a trans femme theatre artist, actor/performer, and musician/sound designer. Excited by fusing lines between emotions, genres, and mediatic forms, Ashley’s creative process uses a post-modern, kaleidoscopic approach influenced by remix culture, blending digital+embodied motifs to evoke feelings of synesthesia. She releases music independently via bandcamp at cloudj.bandcamp.com.

name: Secondsight
bio: Hajia Maa (Secondsight) is a multidisciplinary creative, singer, and a meditation teacher. In her current artistic universe, she mostly explores music, wellness, and fashion, intertwining meditative chant, experimental soul, and ambient soundscapes, sometimes accompanied by guided breathwork, along with a perspective on clothing in which garments are seen as wearable art and a ritual of care for the body and spirit. Her performances invite deep listening, rest, and embodied spiritual intimacy.

name: Tax Haven
bio: Tax Haven is an electronic musician and artist living and working in Toronto, Canada. His work spans complex beat driven post-techno and footwork generated with Max/MSP to shifting and ecstatic ambient compositions making extensive use of sampled youtube audio, the sound of skipping cds, and broken MIDI sequences. As a live performer he uses improvisation and chance based systems to embrace happy accidents and lethal mistakes in equal measure.

name: James Hamilton Lowrie
bio: James Lowrie is a multidisciplinary composer and performer whose work experiments with the intersections of pop and classical traditions. A regular contributor to CBC and Tempo Magazine as a music critic and educator, he is pursuing a DMA in composition at the University of Toronto. He is also the host and creator of the psychedelic comedy/music talk show Radio Haha.

name: bridge of sand. ( all lowercase with period )
bio: bridge of sand.is a spiritual project that fuses pop, hip hop and sound collage production styles together with poetic lyrics of self reflection and dedication to reach at an absolute essence between all these elements. In an improvisation context, bridge will be showcasing his sound collage style through live looping on an SP 404, with his nocturnal form of sampling still intact.

name: Yun
bio: Yun is a versatile artist, keyboardist and producer based in Toronto, Canada. With a background deeply rooted in jazz, she focused her skills as a session keyboard player, collaborating with numerous artists. Yun's musical journey took her to the University of Toronto, where she delved into the intricacies of music technology, earning her master's degree. Armed with this advanced knowledge, she has since become a composer and producer, crafting compositions that span an array of genres. Despite her diverse roles, Yun remains rooted in her origins as a pianist, gracing the stages of numerous festivals alongside some of Toronto's most talented artists.
